defects.title: Microtubular defects
defects.description: Here you can manage microtubular defects.
defects.formDescription: Use the form below to add a new microtubular defect.
defects.description2: Below you can see the existing microtubular defect categories. You can edit or delete them as needed.

data.title: Data collection
data.paragraph1: We strive to provide the best diagnostic experience possible. To do this, we collect anonymized data about how you interact with our application.
data.paragraph2: By analyzing the interactions you make, we gain valuable insights into areas where our AI model may need to be improved. This allows us to continuously improve its accuracy and efficiency.
data.toggleLabel: I understand and agree to the data collection policy.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.paragraph1: Your feedback is essential to help us improve this application. By answering these questions, you help us understand what works well and what could be enhanced.
feedback.paragraph2: We appreciate your time and insights!
feedback.question1: 1. I would use the app again for this task.
feedback.question2: 2. The app was easy to use.
feedback.question3: 3. It was clear how to perform actions within the app.
feedback.question4: 4. I encountered issues or errors that slowed me down.
feedback.question5: 5. The app’s design was visually appealing and intuitive.
feedback.question6: 6. If I made a mistake, it was easy to fix.
feedback.question7: 7. I found the app unnecessarily complicated.
feedback.question8: 8. I think most people would learn to use this app very quickly.
feedback.question9: 9. I am confident that I knew how to use the app.
feedback.question10: 10. It took me a long time to learn how to start using the app.

reportProblem.title: Report problem
reportProblem.intro: Encountered an issue? Report it here and describe the problem in as much detail as possible.
reportProblem.thanksBody: We will review it and use it to improve the application in the future.

layoutDescription.description: Here is a brief description of the layout of the application.

navigation.description: Navigation is located on top of the application. It provides the following functionalities:

features.ai: AI - Run automatic annotation for selected image.
features.centerImage: Center the image when it's zoomed or moved.
features.selectDefect: Select microtubular defect for annotating. It can also be changed using the number keys on the keyboard.
features.saveProject: Save project - Save current state of the project.
features.exportProject: Export report - Export diagnosis report into CSV file.
features.openSettings: Open settings. Here you can allow data collection,  change language of application, and change of microtubular defects.
features.openDocumentation: Open documentation or user feedback questionnaire.

leftSidebar.description: Left sidebar is located on the left side of the application.
leftSidebar.imageUpload: Images can be uploaded by clicking on the Upload button or drag-and-dropped to the left sidebar.

imageInteractions.click: By clicking on an image, it will be displayed in the main image area.
imageInteractions.rightClick: By right-clicking on an image, you can delete it, mark it as reviewed, unseen, or delete all annotations for that image.

annotationList.header: Each image has a list of annotations with its counts below it.

mainImageArea.description: Canvas is located in the center of the application. It displays the selected image and all the annotations for that image.

annotation.add: By clicking on the image, you can add an annotation with the selected microtubular defect.
annotation.changeDynein: Dynein arms of an annotation can be changed by clicking on the annotation and selecting new dynein arms.
annotation.delete: Annotations can be deleted by right-clicking on them.
annotation.details: Clicking on an annotation will show its details on the right sidebar.
annotation.move: Annotation can be moved by dragging it.
annotation.copyPaste: Annotation can be copied by pressing CTRL+C and pasted by pressing CTRL+V.

zoom.drag: You can drag the image by holding the left mouse button and moving it. You can zoom in and out by scrolling the mouse wheel.

rightSidebar.description: Right sidebar is located on the right side of the application. It is separated into three tabs - Project, Image, Annotation.

projectOverview.description: Tab Project displays general information about the project. Statistics about the project are displayed here. You can write the outcome of the diagnosis and add notes about the project.

imageOverview.description: Tab Image displays general information about the selected image. The image can be set as reviewed, unseen, or deleted. Annotations for the image can be deleted. You can also add notes about the image.

annotationOverview.description: Tab Annotation displays general information about the selected annotation. You can change the microtubular defect and dynein arms of the annotation, or update its parametres.

manualTutorial.title: Manual annotation
manualTutorial.step3: Click on an annotation to change the dynein arm type. Simply click on the point until you’re satisfied with the selection.
manualTutorial.repeat: Repeat these steps until each cilium in every image is annotated.
manualTutorial.newClass: Need a different category? Create your own in the Settings.
manualTutorial.description2: This section explains the steps for manual annotation.
manualTutorial.manualAnnotation: Manual annotation is the process of manually annotating cilia in an image. This is done by marking each cilium with a point.
manualTutorial.attributes: Annotation (point) consists of two attributes: microtubular defect and dynein arms.
manualTutorial.microtubularDefects: Microtubular defects are characterized by the absence or misalignment of microtubules in the cilia. These defects can be indicative of PCD and are important to identify during the diagnostic process. The defect category is represented by the color of the annotation.
manualTutorial.dyneinArms: Dynein arms are protein complexes that help cilia move. Dynein arms are important for the proper function of cilia. The dynein arm type is represented by the mark of the annotation.

shortcuts.title: Key Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Here are the key shortcuts you can use:

annotation.title: Annotation shortcuts
annotation.description: Shortcuts to select microtubular defect can be changed in the Settings.

statistics.title: Statistics
statistics.totalMTDs: Total number of annotated cilia with microtubular defect
statistics.totalDA: Total number of annotated cilia with dynein arms
statistics.totalIDA: Total number of annotated cilia without inner dynein arms
statistics.totalODA: Total number of annotated cilia without outer dynein arms

welcome.title: Welcome to PizzaScan
welcome.description: Inspect pizzas one by one, work faster, stay organized, and cut down on repetitive work.

aiModel.title: AI assistance
aiModel.usage: PizzaScan can suggest initial annotations and highlight regions to inspect, so you spend less time on repetitive pointing and clicking, and more time on review decisions.
aiModel.training: The model was trained on a diverse set of pizza inspection images.
aiModel.evolving: Suggestions are not guaranteed to be perfect: edge cases, unusual toppings, lighting, or occlusion can still be challenging. Treat AI output as a starting point: verify, correct when needed, and share feedback so the system can improve.

experience.title: Usage data & privacy
experience.dataCollection: To improve AI localization and annotation, we'd like your permission to collect anonymized usage data about how you interact with AI features, including how often you use them, whether you accept or modify suggestions, and how you work with generated results.
experience.insights: This data is used only to understand usage patterns and improve our AI features. You can change this preference later in Settings.
experience.agreement: I understand and agree to the collection of anonymized usage data as described above.

layoutTutorial.title: App layout
layoutTutorial.step1: Welcome to the tutorial! This tour highlights the main areas of PizzaScan: top bar, sidebars, and workspace.
layoutTutorial.step2: Use the AI button to automatically annotate the image, and the AI Detection button to detect pizzas on the image and crop them into individual images.
layoutTutorial.step3: Use the Center image button to fit the pizza in view, and the Undo button to reverse your last change.
layoutTutorial.step4: Use these controls to choose the defect or category for annotations, and to adjust point size and opacity so marks are easy to see on the pizza.
layoutTutorial.step5: The left sidebar lists your images; expand an image to see its annotations underneath.
layoutTutorial.step6: The center is your workspace: inspect the pizza, add or adjust annotations, and zoom or pan to see details.
layoutTutorial.step7: The right sidebar has Project, Image, and Annotation tabs for summaries and details. Click a tab to switch.

manualAnnotationTutorial.title: Manual annotation
manualAnnotationTutorial.step1: This short guide walks you through manual annotation on pizza images.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step2: Select an image in the left sidebar to open it.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step3: Pick a defect or category in the top bar (colored selector).
manualAnnotationTutorial.step4: Click on the pizza image to add an annotation.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step5: You can add more than one annotation on the same pizza image.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step6: To edit an annotation, select it and use the Annotation tab on the right to change the defect category and related fields.
manualAnnotationTutorial.step7: To delete an annotation, right-click it, or remove it in the Annotation tab on the right.

aiDetectionTutorial.title: AI Detection
aiDetectionTutorial.step1: This short guide walks you through AI Detection. AI finds individual pizzas on the image and crops each one into a separate sub-image.
aiDetectionTutorial.step2: Sub-images appear in the left sidebar, listed under the original image. Click one to open it in the workspace.
aiDetectionTutorial.step3: Annotate each sub-image just like a regular image. Your annotations are linked to that sub-image.
aiDetectionTutorial.step4: Any annotation you add to a sub-image also appears on the main image. You always have the full picture.
aiDetectionTutorial.step5: You can annotate sub-images manually, or let AI do it automatically using the AI button.

AItutorial.title: AI annotation
AItutorial.step1: This short guide walks you through reviewing AI-suggested annotations on pizza images. You stay in control. Only change what needs changing.
AItutorial.step2: When you run AI annotation, AI suggests a defect category for each pizza on the image.
AItutorial.step3: Each annotation square is color-coded by confidence: green is high, orange is medium, and red means the suggestion most likely needs a change.
AItutorial.step4: Click an annotation square to open a review panel. Change the defect category if needed, then press Accept to keep it or Decline to remove it. Everything else stays as AI suggested.
AItutorial.step5: To edit more details, select the annotation and use the Annotation tab on the right sidebar.

0.title: Step 1: Open the Image
0.description: Select the image you want to annotate by clicking on it in the list of images in the Left Sidebar.

1.title: Step 2: Choose a Microtubular Defect Type
1.description: In the navigation, select a category of defect by clicking the "Choose Microtubular Defect" button. You can also switch categories using the numeric keys.

2.title: Step 3: Create Annotations
2.description: Click on the image to add an annotation. You can place multiple annotations on the same image.

3.title: Step 4: Select Dynein Arm Type
3.description: Click on an annotation to change the dynein arm type. Simply click on the point until you’re satisfied with the selection. Repeat these steps until each cilium in every image is annotated.

4.title: Need to Edit an Annotation?
4.description: Click on the annotation, then select tab "Annotation" in the Right Sidebar to edit the microtubular defect category or dynein arms type.

5.title: Need to Delete an Annotation?
5.description: Click on the annotation, then select tab "Annotation" in the Right Sidebar to delete it. You can also delete an annotation by right-clicking on it.

status.class: Microtubular defect class was successfully created
status.classError: Microtubular defect class was not created
status.export: Report was successfully exported and project was saved
status.zoom: Zoom in and out of image with mouse wheel
status.copy: Annotation copied, press CTRL+V to paste
